title: Adobe Creative Cloud - How Instructors, Student Supervisors, and Faculty Can Provide Student Access for Classes or Campus Jobs
body: Please read carefully to determine the correct form to fill out to get access. Summer 2026 forms are now available Info for Summer 2026 All enrolled students now have access to Adobe Express Higher Education without the need to register for additional access. This includes the features included here: Adobe Express Higher Education features . There is no action needed to get this access. All enrolled UW-Madison students already have access to this. If you are requesting access for a class, please make sure that you will need Adobe applications other than Express Higher Education! Thank you for helping us limit Creative Cloud license usage to necessary cases. Grad Students There are two ways to get access: If they are working on a UW-owned computer, Shared Device Licensing may be the best option if multiple users can use the computer. Adobe Shared Device License - Installation and Usage Instructions If they are working on a personal computer or unable to use Shared Device Licensing, read below. Grad students who need Adobe for instruction/class preparation, research, scholarship, or for preparing their dissertation/thesis can now register for access for the academic year through this link: Summer 2026 Grad Student Request Form (make sure you are logged into your UW-Madison Google account). Class Registration Please only submit this form if you are the instructor or a TA for the course, they will be contacted! If your class is a grad student only class, you may want to use the grad student form above to continue access for a full academic year. Students are able to register for access once the class is added. Summer 2026 Class Registration Form (make sure you are logged into your UW-Madison Google account): Student Workers NOTE: Only currently enrolled UW-Madison students are eligible to get licensing through this program. Please help us free up licenses if your student worker no longer needs access: email adobe@g-groups.wisc.edu with the NetIDs that no longer need access There are two ways to get access: If they are working on a UW-owned computer, Shared Device Licensing may be the best option if multiple users can use the computer. Adobe Shared Device License - Installation and Usage Instructions If they are working on a personal computer or unable to use Shared Device Licensing, register here: The priority for these licenses is for classes and instructional preparation but we have a small pool of licenses for student workers. Summer 2026 Student Worker Request Form (make sure you are logged into your UW-Madison Google account): If Semester Access is Ending: Any students scheduled to lose access have been contacted by email. For students to continue their access, they must be in an enrolled class and register again following the directions in this document: Adobe Creative Cloud - Student Subscription FAQ . They can also be registered as a student worker if they have a campus job that requires Adobe Creative Cloud access. See the Student Worker section above. Alternative Access for Ineligible Students: Students will also be able to access Adobe Creative Cloud, Acrobat Pro, and a variety of other software in computer labs across campus . For students interested in purchasing Adobe Creative Cloud for their own personal computer, Adobe offers a discounted price . See Also Adobe Shared Device License - Installation and Usage Instructions Eligibility for Adobe Creative Cloud Licensed By University of Wisconsin System's ETLA Adobe Creative Cloud - Logging in with Your UW-Madison Account Adobe Creative Cloud - Student Subscription FAQ Adobe Creative Cloud at UW-Madison
